summary: Second targeted systems_architect re-review accepts the no-code negation fix. hasNegatedPolicyCue() no longer treats no-code/no code product descriptors as policy negation, while still ignoring explicit safety-policy phrases such as do not build gambling tools. The QA finding for no-code output-only unsafe or regulated LLM drift is closed from an architecture perspective.
review_focus: hasNegatedPolicyCue(), hasNegatedPromiseCue(), policyTopicMatchesText(), and findPolicyPatternMatches() behavior.
Regression tests for no-code unsafe/regulated output drift and explicit safety-policy non-regression.
Updated implementation handoff and latest QA REQUEST_CHANGES handoff.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: QA no-code finding: no-code/no code descriptors do not suppress unsafe or regulated output-topic classification.
acceptance_criteria_review.notes: The negation regex now excludes no-code/no code from the bare no negation path. Regression tests verify no-code sports betting output is excluded and no-code legal compliance output is high-risk checklist framed.
Explicit safety-policy lines such as Do not build gambling tools and Avoid legal compliance guarantees are still ignored as policy cautions rather than treated as the opportunity topic.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: No product scope creep during fix.
acceptance_criteria_review.notes: The reviewed fix is limited to local risk-classification helpers and focused tests. No provider setup, process.env read, network call, database write, API/UI/orchestrator/scoring work, or persistence was introduced.
Implementation and QA handoffs validate. git diff --check, npm test, and npm run build all exit 0.

decisions: Accepted the no-code negation fix as architecturally correct for the QA follow-up because product descriptors no longer disable policy matching.
Closed the no-code QA finding from systems_architect review because the behavior is directly covered by new regression tests and validation passes.
Kept prior M3/M4 boundary decision unchanged: scoreHints remain hints only, with no calculateOpportunityScore(), score persistence, orchestrator, API, or database writes.
Did not edit product files during this re-review; only this systems_architect handoff JSON was updated.
risks: Risk classification remains regex-based and should continue accumulating edge-case tests before real-provider use.
npm run build passes but still emits the known nonfatal local Next SWC native-package warning.
The worktree contains unrelated JustGoal state/message files and untracked reviewer handoffs; they were not changed or reverted.

tests_run.result: PASS; Git printed LF-to-CRLF normalization warnings only.
PASS: 32 tests across 7 suites, including no-code unsafe/regulated output drift and safety-policy non-regression coverage.
PASS: Next.js production build completed successfully with nonfatal local @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc native binary warnings.

requested_action: Record systems_architect ACCEPT after the no-code QA fix and continue lead orchestration or remaining reviewer routing.
